decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.2°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). The strategy shifts to increasing the heat spreader's cross-sectional area to improve lateral spreading and vertical conduction path. We will increase the spreader height by ~14% (within the 1.5x step limit) to provide more material for heat transport without violating geometric bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader height adds thermal mass and cross-sectional area for heat conduction, helping to lower the peak chip temperature now that conductivity is maxed out.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved heat distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Ensure the increased height (0.16) plus y0 (0.32) does not exceed the design domain height (0.5); current sum is 0.48, so it is safe.
Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
